fields.corpus_coverage: Sufficient for an evaluation-focused, multi-turn-conversation-focused recommendation grounded in the two surveys; insufficient for a general LLM-based-agents literature recommendation beyond the evaluation sub-area.
fields.in_corpus: Only the two evaluation surveys are in the user-visible corpus; all other cited/influential agent papers appear only as references within those surveys and are not in the inventory as standalone items.
fields.scope: Evaluation of LLM-based agents, including core capabilities (planning, tool use, self-reflection, memory), application-specific benchmarks (web, SWE, scientific, conversational), generalist agent benchmarks, and multi-turn conversation evaluation.

markdown:
1. Recommendations are scoped to the evaluation of LLM-based agents, covering sub-areas such as core capabilities (planning, tool use, self-reflection, memory), application-specific agent benchmarks (web, SWE, scientific, conversational), generalist agent benchmarks, and multi-turn conversation evaluation. [1][2][3][4][5][6]
2. Only the two evaluation surveys themselves are present in the user-visible corpus: "A Survey on Evaluation of LLM-based Agents" and "Evaluating LLM-based Agents for Multi-Turn Conversations: A Survey." The other works cited or discussed in these surveys (e.g., AgentBench, HAL, MTU-Bench, MMDU, API-Bank, BotChat, HUMOD, Gaia, MetaTool) appear only as references within those surveys and are not in the inventory as standalone items. [7][8][9][10]
3. The corpus of two evaluation-focused surveys is sufficient to ground a narrower recommendation set on the evaluation of LLM-based agents and multi-turn conversational agents, but it is not sufficient to ground a general "important papers on LLM-based agents" recommendation covering the broader literature (planning, tool use, memory, multi-agent systems, general agent architectures) outside the evaluation sub-area. [10][5][11][8][12][9]

Sources
[1] A Survey on Evaluation of LLM-based Agents, Abstract, p. 1
[2] A Survey on Evaluation of LLM-based Agents, 1 Introduction, p. 1
[3] A Survey on Evaluation of LLM-based Agents, 4 Generalist Agent Evaluation, p. 5
[4] A Survey on Evaluation of LLM-based Agents, 4 Generalist Agent Evaluation, p. 5
[5] Evaluating LLM-based Agents for Multi-Turn Conversations: A Survey, Evaluating LLM-based Agents for Multi-Turn Conversations: A Survey, p. 1
[6] Evaluating LLM-based Agents for Multi-Turn Conversations: A Survey, 2.1 Evaluating the End-to-end Experience of LLM-based Agents For Multi-Turn Conversation, p. 6
[7] A Survey on Evaluation of LLM-based Agents, 4 Generalist Agent Evaluation, p. 5
[8] Evaluating LLM-based Agents for Multi-Turn Conversations: A Survey, 2.2 Evaluating The Action and Tool-use Components of LLM-based Agents for Multi-turn Conversation, p. 8
[9] Evaluating LLM-based Agents for Multi-Turn Conversations: A Survey, 4.1 Trends and Status-quo, p. 22
[10] A Survey on Evaluation of LLM-based Agents, A Literature Review Methodology, p. 20
[11] Evaluating LLM-based Agents for Multi-Turn Conversations: A Survey, Evaluating LLM-based Agents for Multi-Turn Conversations: A Survey, p. 1
[12] Evaluating LLM-based Agents for Multi-Turn Conversations: A Survey, 3.2 Evaluation Metrics, p. 17
summary: Recommendations are scoped to the evaluation of LLM-based agents, covering sub-areas such as core capabilities (planning, tool use, self-reflection, memory), application-specific agent benchmarks (web, SWE, scientific, conversational), generalist agent benchmarks, and multi-turn conversation evaluat
